chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presented with sudden onset of severe shortness of breath and chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) and had undergone pulmonary endarterectomy. She had been experiencing worsening symptoms over the past few weeks.
exam: Vital signs: BP 90/60 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 30 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed bilateral crackles and decreased breath sounds.
labs: ECG: showing signs of right heart strain. Bloods: Hb 8 g/dL, platelets 150 x 10^9/L. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram showing massive pulmonary hemorrhage.
correct_diagnosis: Pulmonary hemorrhage after pulmonary endarterectomy in a patient with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension
differentials: Pulmonary hemorrhage after pulmonary endarterectomy in a patient with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension
correct_ranking: Pulmonary hemorrhage after pulmonary endarterectomy in a patient with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension
